title: Exercises: Version Control for Quantum Projects
description: Hands-on exercises to practice version control strategies for quantum software

exercises.title: Implement Circuit Versioning
exercises.description: Create a circuit versioning system that generates unique hashes, tracks metadata, and supports serialization to JSON format suitable for version control.
exercises.task: Complete the CircuitVersion class with hash generation and JSON serialization methods.

    def _compute_hash(self) -> str:
        """
        Compute SHA256 hash of the circuit QASM.
        Return first 12 characters of the hex digest.
        """
        # Your implementation here

    def to_json(self) -> str:
        """
        Serialize to pretty-printed JSON string.
        """
        # Your implementation here

    @classmethod

    def is_same_circuit(self, other: 'CircuitVersion') -> bool:

exercises.solution:
import hashlib
import json
from datetime import datetime
from dataclasses import dataclass, asdict, field
from typing import Dict, Any, Optional

@dataclass
class CircuitVersion:
    """Versioned quantum circuit with metadata."""
    name: str
    version: str
    qasm: str
    num_qubits: int
    depth: int
    created_at: str
    circuit_hash: str = ""
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    
    def __post_init__(self):
        if not self.circuit_hash:
            self.circuit_hash = self._compute_hash()
    
    def _compute_hash(self) -> str:
        """
        Compute SHA256 hash of the circuit QASM.
        Return first 12 characters of the hex digest.
        """
        hash_obj = hashlib.sha256(self.qasm.encode('utf-8'))
        return hash_obj.hexdigest()[:12]
    
    def to_json(self) -> str:
        """
        Serialize to pretty-printed JSON string.
        """
        return json.dumps(asdict(self), indent=2)
    
    @classmethod
    def from_json(cls, json_str: str) -> 'CircuitVersion':
        """
        Deserialize from JSON string.
        """
        data = json.loads(json_str)
        return cls(**data)
    
    def is_same_circuit(self, other: 'CircuitVersion') -> bool:
        """
        Check if two versions represent the same circuit (same hash).
        """
        return self.circuit_hash == other.circuit_hash
hints: Use hashlib.sha256() to compute the hash
Call .hexdigest() to get the hex string, then slice first 12 characters
Use dataclasses.asdict() to convert to dictionary for JSON serialization
Two circuits are the same if they have the same hash, regardless of name or version

exercises.title: Build an Experiment Tracker
exercises.description: Create an experiment tracking system that records all parameters, checkpoints, and metadata needed for reproducing quantum experiments.
exercises.task: Implement the ExperimentTracker class with methods for starting experiments, recording checkpoints, and generating reproducibility reports.
exercises.starterCode:

exercises.solution:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional
from datetime import datetime
import json
import uuid

@dataclass
class Checkpoint:
    iteration: int
    parameters: List[float]
    cost: float
    timestamp: str

@dataclass 
class Experiment:
    experiment_id: str
    name: str
    circuit_hash: str
    backend: str
    shots: int
    seed: Optional[int]
    started_at: str
    completed_at: Optional[str] = None
    checkpoints: List[Checkpoint] = field(default_factory=list)
    final_cost: Optional[float] = None
    git_commit: Optional[str] = None

class ExperimentTracker:
    def __init__(self):
        self.experiments: Dict[str, Experiment] = {}
        self.current_experiment: Optional[str] = None
    
    def start_experiment(
        self,
        name: str,
        circuit_hash: str,
        backend: str,
        shots: int = 1024,
        seed: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> str:
        """
        Start a new experiment and return its ID.
        """
        experiment_id = str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
        
        # Try to get git commit
        git_commit = None
        try:
            import subprocess
            git_commit = subprocess.check_output(
                ['git', 'rev-parse', 'HEAD'],
                stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL
            ).decode().strip()[:8]
        except Exception:
            pass
        
        experiment = Experiment(
            experiment_id=experiment_id,
            name=name,
            circuit_hash=circuit_hash,
            backend=backend,
            shots=shots,
            seed=seed,
            started_at=datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            git_commit=git_commit
        )
        
        self.experiments[experiment_id] = experiment
        self.current_experiment = experiment_id
        
        return experiment_id
    
    def add_checkpoint(
        self,
        iteration: int,
        parameters: List[float],
        cost: float
    ):
        """
        Add a checkpoint to the current experiment.
        """
        if self.current_experiment is None:
            raise RuntimeError("No active experiment")
        
        checkpoint = Checkpoint(
            iteration=iteration,
            parameters=parameters,
            cost=cost,
            timestamp=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        )
        
        self.experiments[self.current_experiment].checkpoints.append(checkpoint)
    
    def complete_experiment(self, final_cost: float):
        """
        Mark current experiment as complete.
        """
        if self.current_experiment is None:
            raise RuntimeError("No active experiment")
        
        exp = self.experiments[self.current_experiment]
        exp.completed_at = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
        exp.final_cost = final_cost
        self.current_experiment = None
    
    def get_reproducibility_info(self, experiment_id: str) -> Dict:
        """
        Get all info needed to reproduce an experiment.
        """
        if experiment_id not in self.experiments:
            raise KeyError(f"Experiment {experiment_id} not found")
        
        exp = self.experiments[experiment_id]
        
        return {
            'experiment_id': exp.experiment_id,
            'name': exp.name,
            'circuit_hash': exp.circuit_hash,
            'backend': exp.backend,
            'shots': exp.shots,
            'seed': exp.seed,
            'git_commit': exp.git_commit,
            'started_at': exp.started_at,
            'completed_at': exp.completed_at,
            'total_iterations': len(exp.checkpoints),
            'final_cost': exp.final_cost,
            'initial_parameters': exp.checkpoints[0].parameters if exp.checkpoints else None,
            'final_parameters': exp.checkpoints[-1].parameters if exp.checkpoints else None
        }

testCases.input:
tracker = ExperimentTracker()
exp_id = tracker.start_experiment('test', 'abc', 'sim', seed=42)
tracker.complete_experiment(0.5)
info = tracker.get_reproducibility_info(exp_id)
info['seed']

hints: Use uuid.uuid4() and slice the first 8 characters for experiment ID
Store current_experiment ID to know which experiment to update
Use subprocess to get git commit hash, but handle exceptions
Include both initial and final parameters in reproducibility info

exercises.title: Create a Semantic Version Manager

hints: Use regex to parse the version string with optional prerelease
When bumping major, reset minor and patch to 0
Prerelease versions (e.g., -beta) come before the release version
Two versions are compatible if they have the same major version

exercises.title: Design a Git LFS Configuration
exercises.description: Create a proper .gitattributes file for a quantum project that uses Git LFS for large files while keeping circuit definitions as regular text files.
exercises.task: Write the .gitattributes content that properly configures Git LFS tracking for a quantum project.
exercises.starterCode:
def generate_gitattributes() -> str:
    """
    Generate .gitattributes content for a quantum project.
    
    Requirements:
    - Track .npy, .npz, .hdf5, .h5 files with LFS
    - Track .pkl and .pickle files with LFS  
    - Track .qpy (Qiskit binary) files with LFS
    - Track all files in data/results/ with LFS
    - Keep .qasm files as regular text
    - Keep .json files as regular text
    - Track large images (.png, .pdf) with LFS
    
    Returns:
        String content for .gitattributes file
    """
    # Your implementation here
    # Each line should be: pattern filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
    # Or: pattern text (for text files)
    pass
exercises.solution:
def generate_gitattributes() -> str:
    """
    Generate .gitattributes content for a quantum project.
    """
    lines = [
        "# Git LFS Configuration for Quantum Projects",
        "",
        "# Large numerical data",
        "*.npy filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
        "*.npz filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
        "*.hdf5 filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
        "*.h5 filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
        "",
        "# Serialized objects",
        "*.pkl filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
        "*.pickle filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
        "*.qpy filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
        "",
        "# Results directory",
        "data/results/** filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
        "",
        "# Keep circuit definitions as text for diffing",
        "*.qasm text",
        "circuits/**/*.json text",
        "",
        "# Large images",
        "*.png filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
        "*.pdf filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text",
    ]
    
    return "\n".join(lines)
hints: LFS tracked files use: pattern filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
Order doesn't matter, but grouping similar patterns improves readability
